events.title: Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan
events.description: More than 1 million people advised to evacuate homes amid 80mph winds and heavy rain Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) moved northwards over the course of this week. From Okinawa to mainland Japan, prolonged and heavy rainfall led to landslide warnings and the flooding of rivers, with Japan issuing level 4 warnings for some rivers, signalling a risk of overflowing. This level is high enough for municipalities to issue evacuation orders. Three-hourly rainfall totals on Wednesday reached 105mm in Chiyoda, Tokyo, which was a record high for the month. Sustained wind speeds of 80mph (130kph) were recorded on Monday – making it a category 1 typhoon – bringing damage and disruption to businesses, transport, infrastructure and the environment. By Wednesday, 23 people had been injured, 17 of whom were in Okinawa. The typhoon damaged 57 homes and led to 60,000 homes losing electricity. In addition to this, 1.52 million people were advised to evacuate by authorities. The typhoon damaged the exterior wall of Himeji Castle, a Unesco world heritage site in western Japan. The maximum recorded wind speed at Himeji was 56mph, according to the Japan Meteorological Agency. The typhoon has now weakened into a tropical depression and has moved eastwards, away from the islands. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan

events.title: Feeling the heat: Women and climate change
events.description: In a special edition, we examine how climate change is affecting the lives of women as rising temperatures and increasingly extreme weather events continue to impact communities worldwide. This as the world is also facing a warming El Nino climate pattern in the coming months. We report from India where a series of recent intense heatwaves has pushed temperatures to dangerous levels and posed a particularly serious risk to pregnant women. Annette Young also talks to UK author, Natasha Walter, whose latest book is entitled 'Feminism for a World on Fire,’ who says a feminist approach is badly needed to address the climate crisis. Plus, how for millions of women, heatwaves are not just about the rising temperatures. It also puts them at risk of harassment in public as they wear lighter clothes such as shorts or sleeveless dresses and tops, according to French data.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Feeling the heat: Women and climate change

events.title: Yemen: Hunger crisis deepens as funding cuts leave millions without support
events.description: Nearly half of the population in Government-controlled areas of Yemen are facing high levels of acute food insecurity with the crisis set to deepen further if international aid cuts continue, according to the latest analysis by the leading UN-backed global food security platform.

events.title: Outrage in Argentina after two teen girls murdered as femicide crisis endures
events.description: Country is shaken by the brutal murders of two girls, aged 14 and 17, whose bodies were discovered just days apart Argentina has reacted with fury after the bodies of two murdered teenage girls were found just two days apart. The latest killings underscore the South American country’s enduring femicide crisis despite years of feminist campaigning, and have prompted alarm over the decision to cut support for victims of gender-based violence under the far-right administration of Javier Milei. Police found the remains of Agostina Vega, 14, on Saturday, in a field on the outskirts of the city of Córdoba. She had been fatally strangled and her body had been dismembered, according to local media reports. Continue reading...

events.title: AI feature film on Iran’s protest movement makes festival history
events.description: A film about Iran's protest movement is making cinema history. "Dreams of Violets" is the first fully AI-generated feature film ever selected by a major international film festival. The 75-minute drama will premiere at New York's Tribeca Festival next week. Created by Iranian-British director Ash Koosha from his home in London, the film took just three months to produce and cost less than 2,000 euros. There were no actors, no cameras, no sets and no film crew. Koosha says the film simply could not have been made through conventional means. Living in exile and unable to safely film inside Iran, he turned to AI to recreate events linked to the country's deadly crackdown on anti-government protesters. The film is based on journalistic reports, photographs and eyewitness accounts, and explores themes of memory, censorship and resistance. But as Tribeca becomes the first major festival to embrace a fully AI-generated feature, the film is also reigniting a fierce debate. Can artificial intelligence tell deeply human stories? Does AI democratise filmmaking or threaten the future of the industry? Eve Jackson speaks to Ash Koosha about Iran, ethics and the future of cinema.

events.title: Undeclared chemical weapons found in Syria, including type used in notorious Ghouta massacre
events.description: Chemical weapons inspectors have uncovered a significant cache of previously undeclared chemical weapons in Syria – including rockets of the same type used in the notorious 2013 Ghouta attack – in what the UN’s top disarmament official called a “momentous discovery” for international security.

events.title: ‘Family values’ African charter condemned by rights groups as regressive and dangerous
events.description: Draft treaty claims sexual and reproductive health and rights are an existential threat to the African family An African treaty that rejects longstanding international human rights obligations moved a step closer to becoming policy this week as governments across the continent met in Ghana. The draft African charter on family, sovereignty and values, seen by the Guardian, asserts that African values and culture are under attack from “foreign ideologies” and urges states to withdraw from any agreements that do not align with the principles of the charter, including the 2003 Maputo protocol , which promotes gender equality and protects the reproductive and health rights of women and girls. Continue reading...

events.title: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response

At least 10 people were massacred in raids on three villages around the city of Beni, in North Kivu, in the early hours of Wednesday morning. Continue reading...

events.title: Nauru issues rare statement after whistleblower alleges violent threats against Australia’s non-citizens
events.description: Nauran government says its citizens are ‘friendly’ after MP spoke of serious threats allegedly made against former detainees Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Nauruan government has issued a rare statement insisting it is a “friendly” and “welcoming” country after a whistleblower alleged “serious threats of physical violence” were made against a group of non-citizens removed there by the Albanese government. The unexpected defence, sent shortly after midday on Thursday, was mounted hours after the independent MP Andrew Wilkie used his three-minute constituency statement to read claims from an anonymous whistleblower familiar with the arrangements of the secretive $2.5bn deal between Australia and Nauru. Continue reading...

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
events.description: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters

Judge Amy Lee ordered the removal of Levi Mendez-Maldonado in absentia on 21 May. Mendez-Maldonado, originally from Honduras, came to the United States as an unaccompanied minor at age 17 and was murdered in a shooting in November 2024. Continue reading...

events.title: Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens
events.description: The Middle East is Japan’s main source of crude oil, from which naphtha is extracted and used to make items including printing ink and plastics Takeaways, supermarkets, and bakeries in Japan are running out of plastic bags, trays and food service gloves amid widening shortages of the key plastic ingredient, naphtha, due to the Middle East crisis. The food sector accounts for nearly one-third of Japan’s annual plastic use of more than 8m tonnes, and price rises and shortages are hitting hard across the industry and beyond. Some outlets have begun offering perks to customers who bring their own bags, plates or containers. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens

events.title: Turkmenistan's 'heavenly' Akhal-Teke horses celebrated in annual beauty pageants
events.description: The Central Asian country of Turkmenistan is immensely proud of its horses. The ancient and endangered Akhal-Teke species has become a symbol of great national pride in a deeply isolated state where power is firmly concentrated, and are considered so beautiful that beauty pageants are held for them annually. The species and the culture surrounding them has even been listed on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage List.

events.title: Allegra Spender defends voting against CGT reform – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jane Hume , the deputy opposition leader, accused the government of being “sneaky”, saying discussions around tax reforms in parliament were much too short to properly address so-called “generational” tax reforms. “What the government has done is essentially prevented scrutiny of the changes that this legislation is going to inflict on the Australian economy,” Hume told RN Breakfast. She went on: Apparently, these are generational reforms. If they’re generational reforms, well, surely they should have been taken to an election so that the Australian people could decide that. Two days simply is not enough. There is no need to rush these changes through because they don’t kick in until 2028. Victim-survivors and their loved ones will no longer be forced to sit in court and hear the person convicted of a heinous crime be described as an otherwise good person. While I’m glad we’re finally here, it should not have taken so long for the Liberals and Nationals to listen to victim-survivors and their advocates and drop their opposition to these reforms. This win belongs to every survivor in this country. … That is hard-won dignity. But I’m not done. Every survivor in every corner of this country deserves the same protection, and I will fight until every jurisdiction reflects that. NSW was first. The rest will follow. Continue reading...

events.title: Woman illegally strip-searched by police ‘reasonably thought’ officers would call her a liar, judge says
events.description: NSW seeking leave to appeal against successful class action including aggravated damages of $20,000 awarded to woman strip-searched at music festival Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The woman awarded $93,000 after an illegal strip-search by NSW police thought until “just before” the trial that she would be called a liar in the proceedings by the officers who conducted the search. This was despite the officers submitting statements 12 months before her appearance that they had no recollection of the woman or the search, a court was told on Friday. Lawyers acting for the woman, Raya Meredith, and 6,000 music festivalgoers appeared before the NSW court of appeal on Friday in the final day of a two-day hearing where NSW is seeking leave to appeal after losing a class action over unlawful strip-searches conducted at music festivals between 2018 and 2022. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Woman illegally strip-searched by police ‘reasonably thought’ officers would call her a liar, judge says

Incomprehension has grown nationwide after it emerged the suspect had twice previously been formally accused of raping a child, but the investigations had either been dropped or stalled. events.title: Cameroon faces major drinking water shortages despite boosted production
events.description: In Cameroon, national drinking water production is estimated at 1.17 million cubic meters per day, according to Camwater, through its 103 stations spread across the country. Despite this volume, the drinking water deficit remains very significant in major cities. In Yaounde, demand exceeds 300,000 cubic meters per day, while supply is capped at 215,000 cubic meters . As a result, for thousands of households, obtaining drinking water is a real struggle.

events.title: Experts criticise plan for American-only Ebola quarantine centre in Kenya
events.description: Plan departs from policy of bringing CDC staff back to US for treatment and offering support to all health workers Former top US officials and other experts are urging the Trump administration to abandon plans for an Ebola quarantine and treatment centre in Kenya, as the union for workers with the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) calls for Americans exposed to Ebola to be brought home for treatment. Soon after the US revealed it was setting up a field hospital in Kenya for the Ebola quarantine and treatment of Americans, the Kenyan high court blocked the order – but the Kenyan and US governments moved forward anyway, with the first American responders reportedly landing at the Laikipia airbase on Saturday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Experts criticise plan for American-only Ebola quarantine centre in Kenya

events.title: Canada endorses embattled marine park’s plan to relocate 30 beluga whales
events.description: Beluga whales, which Marineland threatened to euthanize in 2025, will be moved to sanctuaries in Spain or across US Canada and an embattled marine park have reached a tentative deal on the future of 30 beluga whales , ending a saga that has captivated the public and angered animal rights groups. The federal fisheries ministry announced this week that all of Marineland’s belugas would be shipped to either Spain or one of four locations in the US , ending whale captivity in Canada. Continue reading...

events.title: India: Viral 'Cockroach' party to take message to the streets
events.description: India’s Cockroach Janta Party started as an online youth movement, taking aim at the country’s government over high unemployment and inflation. But after gaining huge traction online, it is now set to take its message to the streets with a protest planned for this Saturday in New Delhi. Its leader, currently based in the US, has planned to return to India for the demonstration, even if he fears being arrested.

events.title: India's 'Cockroach Party' chief flies to New Delhi for protest
events.description: The founder of India's satirical online "Cockroach People's Party" said he was flying back to New Delhi on Friday, to take the viral social media campaign to the streets. The parody "Cockroach Janta Party" (CJP) – echoing Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Hindu-nationalist Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) – has won millions of online followers on social media since its launch last month.

events.title: Bryan Kohberger's victims 'endured a high degree of pain' and suffering, unsealed autopsy reports show
events.description: Unsealed autopsy reports reveal University of Idaho victims suffered multiple sharp-force injuries consistent with a Ka-Bar knife in the 2022 attack.

events.title: John Gotti's grandson allegedly attacked girlfriend days before reporting to prison for COVID fraud
events.description: John Gotti's grandson Carmine Gotti Agnello allegedly attacked his girlfriend days before reporting to prison for stealing COVID-19 relief funds.

events.title: Zelenskyy issues open letter to Putin proposing meeting as US 'fully focused' on Iran
events.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposes meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin to negotiate end of years-long Russia-Ukraine war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zelenskyy issues open letter to Putin proposing meeting as US 'fully focused' on Iran

events.title: Mexico World Cup 2026 preview: Players to watch, group matches and squad
events.description: El Tri lost in the last 16 in seven consecutive World Cups but have higher hopes for the 2026 tournament.

events.title: Shakira and Burna Boy opening World Cup in Mexico, Katy Perry headlines US
events.description: Katy Perry will front the opening US World Cup game, while Alanis Morissette and Michael Buble will open Canada's games.

events.title: Ranking the World Cup 2026 groups: Which teams are favourites to progress?
events.description: The first stage of the 2026 World Cup 2026 features 12 groups. We've ranked them and picked the favourites to advance.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 talking points: NFL-style half-time show, kits and more
events.description: Al Jazeera breaks down the key talking points ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 which kicks off next week.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
events.description: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

events.title: 'A parallel America': Obama's $850m presidential centre offers a vision of hope
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, June 5: The press pays tribute to French-Iranian graphic novelist and filmmaker Marjane Satrapi, whose work transformed perceptions of Iran around the world. Best known for her landmark graphic memoir Persepolis, Satrapi helped redefine the comic book as a medium for political and personal storytelling. Also, journalists have been given a preview of Barack Obama's presidential centre in Chicago ahead of its opening later this month. And Prince Andrew is back in the tabloids following fresh revelations about his housing arrangements.

events.title: FIFA 'regrets' free World Cup tickets and demands fans pay up
events.description: FIFA has confirmed a website glitch has left dozens of fans getting free tickets for the World Cup 2026.

events.title: Historic Big Boy steam locomotive draws crowds as America 250 tour heads east
events.description: Union Pacific's Big Boy No. 4014, the world's largest operating steam locomotive, is drawing crowds as it makes its first East Coast tour ahead of America's 250th birthday.

events.title: Zelensky proposes face-to-face talks in open letter to Putin
events.description: Ukraine's president tells the Russian leader that only "direct engagement" between the two countries could end the war, with the US focused on Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zelensky proposes face-to-face talks in open letter to Putin

events.title: Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon
events.description: The United States announced the ceasefire agreement on Wednesday night following a fresh round of talks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon

events.title: US actor James Handy stabbed to death, with girlfriend's son arrested
events.description: Handy, 81, is known for his roles in films including Jumanji and Top Gun: Maverick.

events.title: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas
events.description: Micro dramas have surged in popularity, but drawn criticism for often sensationalist content.

events.title: How the US naval blockade has bled Iran of nearly $6bn in oil revenues
events.description: Iran is exporting less than one-sixth of the oil it was shipping before the war began.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How the US naval blockade has bled Iran of nearly $6bn in oil revenues

events.title: Zelenskyy asks Putin for meeting: What’s he offering, could Russia accept?
events.description: Russia and Ukraine have been holding peace talks since the war began in 2022, but with no concrete outcome.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zelenskyy asks Putin for meeting: What’s he offering, could Russia accept?

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: ANU scandals during Julie Bishop’s tenure caused $100m in reputational damage, university estimates
events.description: Interim vice-chancellor tells senate estimates of ‘very significant’ effect on donor pipeline and enrolments Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A series of high profile scandals and governance failures has cost the Australian National University $100m in reputational damage, the interim vice-chancellor, Rebekah Brown, has told a parliamentary committee. Fronting senate estimates on Friday – a day after a scathing audit report found the ANU approved a controversial $250m cost-cutting program without clear evidence – Brown acknowledged it had been a challenging time for the university. Continue reading...

events.title: Protesters in Albania oppose plan for Trump family-linked resort
events.description: Protesters in Albania oppose Jared Kushner's planned Adriatic coast resort, which would be located in one of the Mediterranean's most environmentally sensitive areas.

events.title: Germany's Iran war lessons after 100 days
events.description: The war waged by the US and Israel against Iran has now lasted 100 days. In that time, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has drastically changed his stance on the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany's Iran war lessons after 100 days

events.title: Slovenia's political survivor Janez Jansa is back
events.description: The new coalition government of Slovenia's veteran leader Janez Jansa is already advancing divisive policies that some warn could deepen polarization.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Slovenia's political survivor Janez Jansa is back

events.title: Ukraine: Marine drone explodes in Romanian port
events.description: A naval drone exploded in the Romanian port of Constanta on Friday morning, causing no injuries. Ukraine's navy claimed ownership of the device which it said had been "jammed" by Russian electronic warfare. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Marine drone explodes in Romanian port

events.title: Mali junta offers $3.5M reward for Sahel al-Qaeda leader
events.description: Mali's military government is offering $3.5 million for information leading to the arrest or killing of Iyad Ag Ghaly. The junta is also offering ​smaller rewards for other suspects, including Tuareg separatists.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mali junta offers $3.5M reward for Sahel al-Qaeda leader

events.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy proposes Putin meeting on ending war
events.description: The leaders of Russia and Ukraine have both signaled confidence, and willingness to talk. Putin shows no sign of backing off from his demands, while Zelenskyy said Ukraine would keep fighting for "survival."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine's Zelenskyy proposes Putin meeting on ending war

events.title: EU should not miss an opportunity on Ukraine diplomacy: Bulgarian FM Velislava Petrova
events.description: Bulgaria has become notorious for holding elections. In 5 years, voters went to the polls 8 times. But this debilitating crisis finally ended on April 19th, when the Progressive Bulgaria party won an outright majority. It is led by the former president, Rumen Radev, now the prime minister.

events.title: Ghana hosts 4th African inter-Parliamentary conference on family values
events.description: Ghana is hosting the 4th African inter-Parliamentary conference on family values a week after the country passed a punitive bill to criminalise LGBTQ activities. The country’s speaker of Parliament said Ghana is being blackmailed by western countries with aid money to stop it from protecting its traditional values. FRANCE 24’s Justice Baidoo reports.

events.title: Missing Sherpa crawls back to Mount Everest base camp
events.description: A Sherpa guide has crawled back to base camp on Mount Everest a week after going missing.

events.title: From the Field: In crisis-hit Middle East, renewables power daily life
events.description: Across the Middle East, countries facing severe energy access challenges are turning to renewables to power essential infrastructure, from hospitals to schools and street lighting.

events.title: Handcuffed suspect allegedly slips restraints, steals Dallas police cruiser with officer trapped in back seat
events.description: A handcuffed suspect allegedly slipped his restraints, commandeered a Dallas police cruiser and jumped out of it at approximately 50 mph, authorities said.

events.title: Karmelo Anthony defense, prosecutors wage first-impression war as emotions rock opening of trial: expert
events.description: Karmelo Anthony's murder trial opens with emotional 911 call, surveillance video and witnesses describing efforts to save stabbing victim Austin Metcalf.

events.title: Army vet dad running for sheriff gets murder charge dismissed in shooting of daughter's alleged predator

events.ticker_label: 🚨 Army vet dad running for sheriff gets murder charge dismissed in shooting of daughter's alleged predator

events.title: Family of Los Alamos lab worker found dead near handgun rejects suicide theory, suspects foul play
events.description: Melissa Casias vanished from Taos, New Mexico, in June 2025. Her family and a private investigator question circumstances of her death near a handgun.

events.title: Coast Guard reels in dinghy used by Brian and Lynette Hooker before Bahamas disappearance
events.description: U.S. Coast Guard divers search the Sea of Abaco near Elbow Cay in the Bahamas for Lynette Hooker, missing since April after allegedly falling overboard.

events.title: Hezbollah's 'game changing' night-hunting weapon punches through Israel's defenses: expert
events.description: Drone expert warns Hezbollah's nighttime drone capabilities are game changing, using thermal sensors to target IDF troops under cover of darkness.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hezbollah's 'game changing' night-hunting weapon punches through Israel's defenses: expert

events.title: English cops cuffed teen stabbing victim after attacker claimed racial assault
events.description: Police apologize after handcuffing stabbing victim Henry Nowak based on attacker's alleged racism claim, sparking outrage over British policing culture.

events.title: US plans to fight flesh-eating screwworm outbreak with flies and dogs
events.description: The insect, which has been found in America for the first time since 1966, is a growing concern in the southern US.

events.title: Lebanon ceasefire brings little comfort to displaced families in Beirut
events.description: Months after fleeing the fighting in eastern and southern Lebanon, thousands of displaced people remain in makeshift camps across Beirut. Despite the announcement of a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, many say they feel no safer and have little confidence the truce will hold. France 24's special correspondent Antonia Kerrigan
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon ceasefire brings little comfort to displaced families in Beirut

events.title: Fireworks reportedly trigger mass horse stampede through Rome streets, injuring several soldiers
events.description: Unauthorized fireworks reportedly triggered a mass horse stampede during a late-night rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade in Rome, injuring riders.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy,' says they still get along
events.description: The president also acknowledged that he cursed at the Israeli leader in a heated phone call in which he told Benjamin Netanyahu not to bomb the Lebanese capital Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy,' says they still get along

events.title: Greetings from Porto, whose lanes are lined with colorful textiles
events.description: Linen scarves, cotton aprons and dishtowels adorn the entrances to souvenir shops, many of which are run by Bangladeshis whose home country shares Portugal's rich tradition of textile manufacturing.

events.title: Australia seizes 100,000 illegal cockroaches in record bug bust
events.description: The haul of live insects included dubia cockroaches and Madagascar hissing cockroaches. Authorities said it was the country's biggest-ever seizure of illegal exotic invertebrates.

events.title: Trump critic John Bolton to plead guilty in documents case
events.description: Donald Trump's former national security adviser-turned critic is accused of mishandling classified ⁠documents while he wrote an unflattering memoir about Trump's first term.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump critic John Bolton to plead guilty in documents case

events.title: Woman leaps from balcony to escape kidnapping after horrific cross-country ordeal: officials
events.description: A Florida woman allegedly kidnapped by her ex-boyfriend escaped a three-week ordeal by jumping from a California balcony, Lee County authorities say.

events.title: Albino buffalo nicknamed 'Donald Trump' becomes sensation at Bangladesh's national zoo for its blond hair
events.description: A rare albino buffalo nicknamed "Donald Trump" has become a sensation at Bangladesh's national zoo thanks to its blond hair resembling the president.

events.title: Ukraine strikes cargo ships and admits Romania drone blast as Putin prepares for key speech
events.description: Ukraine says it struck five ships carrying illegal cargo in the Sea of Azov and in coastal waters of Russian-occupied territories.

events.title: Nearly 50 people die of thirst in Sahara desert after lorry breaks down
events.description: Only two survived after trekking more than 50km (30 miles) across the desert to alert the authorities.

events.title: Trump hits back at 'unpatriotic' vote after House rebukes him over Iran
events.description: The lower chamber of Congress passed a measure that seeks to halt further military action, in a vote seen as largely symbolic.

events.title: Thousands protest in Albania over Kushner-linked resort project

events.title: Plans for a Trump family-linked resort spark protests in Albania
events.description: A coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner is facing resistance in Albania. The government says the project will transform the nation, but environmental campaigners and critics oppose it.

events.title: Video of a dying teen handcuffed by police sparks outrage and riots across Britain
events.description: There is outrage in the U.K. over the murder of 18-year-old Henry Nowak. Bodycam footage shows police handcuffing him as he lay dying. His killer falsely accused Nowak of racist abuse.

events.description: A massive coastal resort development in Albania linked to President Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner has sparked days of protests.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: US imposes new sanctions on Cuban president and Castro family members
events.description: US secretary of state Marco Rubio says anyone providing services to listed entities ‘is at risk of sanctions themselves’ The United States has announced fresh economic sanctions on Cuba’s president and some of his immediate family, alongside members of the Castro family, in Washington’s latest ramping up of pressure on its communist-led neighbour. Among those targeted were the son and a grandson of former president Raúl Castro, who no longer holds an official position but remains a key figure on decisions about the future of the island. Continue reading...

events.title: Environment wins of the week
events.description: Good news about the state of the planet might seem to be in short supply, but it's out there. Each week, DW's environment desk brings you what went right.

events.title: Negotiations on air passenger rights hit turbulence: EU Parliament takes on airlines
events.description: Negotiators from the European Parliament and EU member states have been trying to reach an agreement on the rights of air passengers travelling within the bloc. The current rules date back to 2004, but the scale and nature of air travel have changed dramatically since then. We take you inside the negotiations, examine the key sticking points, and explain why MEPs are pushing for stronger protections for passengers affected by delays and cancellations.

events.title: Murdaugh murder retrial could crack open sealed court records tied to infamous ‘egg juror’
events.description: SC prosecutors ask the state Supreme Court to unseal secret transcripts tied to the removal of Alex Murdaugh's "egg juror" ahead of his new trial.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Murdaugh murder retrial could crack open sealed court records tied to infamous ‘egg juror’

events.title: Prominent realtor, ex-husband and their daughters, ages 11 and 8, found dead in affluent Miami suburb
events.description: A South Florida real estate agent, her ex-husband, and their two young daughters were found dead with stab wounds in a Doral murder-suicide.

events.title: Anti-cartel candidate 'The Tiger' channels Trump and Bukele in Colombia election shocker
events.description: Analysts say Colombia's presidential election signals growing regional rejection of leftist governments as voters prioritize security and counternarcotics.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Zimbabwe elected to UN Security Council
events.description: The United Nations General Assembly on Wednesday elected ​Austria, Kyrgyzstan, Portugal, Trinidad and Tobago and Zimbabwe to the 15-member UN Security Council for two-year terms starting on January 1, 2027. The Security Council is the only UN body that can make legally binding decisions such as imposing ‌sanctions and authorising use of force. FRANCE 24's Sharon Mazingaizo reports.

events.title: Superdrug removes skincare brand amid concerns over Israeli settlement products
events.description: Superdrug, the UK's second-largest beauty and health retailer, has blocked a skincare brand from its website after suspected cosmetic ingredients were seen at an Israeli settlement in the West Bank.

events.title: New Jersey man accused of killing wife with barbell allegedly confessed in messages to relatives: report
events.description: A New Jersey man allegedly used a barbell to kill his wife and confessed in emails and texts to family, according to a Monmouth County affidavit.

events.title: Canada's Carney pledges action on antisemitism amid backlash over new anti-hate council members
events.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's new council to combat antisemitism draws backlash over members critics say are hostile to Israel and Jewish concerns.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.description: A man suspected of murdering an 11-year-old girl had previously been identified as a potential child molester, records show.

events.title: Ex-wife of Dubai ruler's nephew in custody, prosecutors say

events.description: The run-off follows a tumultuous first round of voting, which saw long lines, delayed results and accusations of fraud.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice

events.title: Europe’s far right exploit Henry Nowak murder in UK with populist rhetoric on race
events.description: Polish, Spanish and French populists focus on clips of teenager’s dying moments and accuse UK of descending ‘into depths of the earth’ UK politics live – latest updates Polish far-right politicians have claimed that the murder of Henry Nowak symbolises “Britain’s descent into the depths of the earth” as populists from France, Spain and Japan focused on harrowing clips of his dying moments. Despite pleas from Nowak’s family for people not to exploit the killing for political gain and to focus on cutting knife crime, their comments have focused on race and immigration. Continue reading...

events.title: One Nation, six farcical explanations and no clearer understanding of its housing policy
events.description: Hanson’s party is leading in the polls, but it has a long way to go before being recognised as a serious political outfit Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When sent out to do a cleanup job, it usually helps to not make the mess even worse. It took One Nation six separate attempts over nearly 24 hours to clarify the basic details of their policy on foreign ownership of housing. Between Thursday night and Friday afternoon, the story turned from bizarre to farcical, with attempts to clarify the policy just making the situation murkier as Australians watched: a Barnaby Joyce interview; a quick do-over on Sky News on Thursday; a Pauline Hanson social media update on Friday morning; a Sean Bell interview, again on Sky, on Friday; and then a 2GB spot; before a written press release from Bell on Friday afternoon Continue reading...

events.title: Peru election could add to string of right-wing victories in Latin America
events.description: Peruvians go to the polls Sunday to elect their next president amid a conservative shift in Latin America. The daughter of a former dictator, a far-right senator, could win.

events.title: US Senate passes $70 billion funding for ICE, Border Patrol
events.description: Senators voted 52-47 to approve funds set to finance the immigration forces for another three years, through the rest of Donald Trump's term. The bill still needs to clear the House; that vote is expected next week.

events.title: EU to advance expansion plans at Balkans summit
events.description: European leaders are gathering in Montenegro with EU expansion high on the agenda. Montenegro is a leading candidate for accession while, further east, Ukraine and Moldova also harbor ambitions.

events.title: India news: Putin hails Delhi ties, Trump calls Modi 'good friend'
events.description: Russian leader Vladimir Putin said that India’s cooperation with the US won't harm India-Russia relations, while US President Donald Trump said he has a "good relationship" with Prime Minister Narendra Modi. DW has more.

events.title: What are Europe's alternatives to Instagram, TikTok and X?
events.description: As criticism of Instagram, TikTok and X grows in the EU, developers have been working on European social media apps. But can they compete? DW answers the key questions.

events.title: Dutch police arrest 4 men over drugging, rape of partners
events.description: The men allegedly filmed the sexual assaults and shared the footage. The case is reminiscent of that of French woman Gisele Pelicot.

events.title: German asylum benefits cuts violate EU law, top court rules
events.description: In a victory for rejected asylum-seekers, the European Court of Justice has ruled they must be given more than just the bare minimum to survive. But as the EU's new migration pact kicks in, will relief be short-lived?

events.title: EU seeking ways to speed up Western Balkans membership
events.description: European Council President Antonio Costa said new ways need to be found to speed up Western Balkans membership in the EU. His comments come ahead of an upcoming summit in Montenegro.

events.title: Why Yad Vashem is coming to Germany
events.description: "Remember" — every child in Israel knows the meaning of this Jewish commandment: Never forget the Holocaust. Now Yad Vashem wants to make sure people in Germany remember it too.

events.title: Thailand: Sexual abuse scandal inside family business empire forces public reckoning
events.description: #Thailand is facing a rare public reckoning over how it deals with matters of sexual #abuse after a member of a family #business empire publicly accused his brother of abusing him when he was younger. Since, other celebrities and public figures have shared their own experiences, calling for an end to #victim-blaming.

events.title: Jerusalem’s Pride and Tolerance march takes on a political tone
events.description: June is gay pride month and Jerusalem’s Pride and Tolerance march took place on Thursday. This year’s march took on a political tone as the parade route took marchers to Israel’s parliament. While LGBT people have more civil rights in Israel than many other countries in the Middle East attendees said there’s still a lot of room for progress.

events.title: Greenland is Denmark's 'for now', says America's top diplomat
events.description: "For now."

events.title: Judge blocks Jewish group's attempt to stop Kanye West gigs
events.description: Kanye West will be allowed to perform two gigs in the Netherlands after a judge shot down a Jewish group's attempt to block them.

events.title: Building trust on patrol: One police officer’s peacekeeping journey in South Sudan
events.description: From a police station in Germany to the UN Mission in South Sudan, Stephanie Königs has been talking to UN News about her journey to becoming a peacekeeper – and the importance of trust and empathy in the field.

events.title: Kyrgyzstan wins seat on UN Security Council for first time ever
events.description: A closely watched Security Council election delivered a mix of continuity and change on Wednesday, as Austria, Portugal, Trinidad and Tobago, Zimbabwe – and first-time member Kyrgyzstan, secured seats around the iconic horseshoe table.

events.title: Democrat bankrolling Graham Platner's campaign ripped for downplaying abuse allegations and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Overcrowded migrant vessel with 240 people on board intercepted near Turks and Caicos
events.description: The U.S. Coast Guard and Customs and Border Protection intercepted an overcrowded migrant vessel taking on water with 240 people on board near Turks and Caicos Islands.

events.title: Feds say US citizen gathered information on American targets for Chinese Communist Party
events.description: Thomas Pauken II pleaded guilty to acting as a Chinese intelligence agent, allegedly gathering info on U.S. targets and infiltrating political circles.

events.title: Woman’s body washes ashore in Florida as investigators dig into beach discovery
events.description: Police are investigating after a woman's body was found on Hollywood Beach, Florida, near North Boardwalk and Thomas Street on Wednesday evening.

events.title: FBI launches 'Most Wanted Fraudsters' list as DOJ charges Ohio defendants in $30M Medicaid scheme for kids
events.description: DOJ unveils major fraud crackdown including a $30 million Ohio Medicaid scheme allegedly funding luxury cars and the FBI's new Most Wanted Fraudsters list.

events.title: Man found with zip ties and duct tape charged with assaulting five women on UCLA campus in under an hour
events.description: A man allegedly sexually assaulted multiple women on the UCLA campus in under an hour and was found with zip ties and duct tape, prosecutors say.

events.title: Trump expands Cuba sanctions beyond US companies in major crackdown on foreign enablers
events.description: Trump administration rolls out unprecedented secondary sanctions on Cuba targeting foreign firms doing business with military-linked conglomerate GAESA.

events.title: Bodycam footage fuels backlash after police handcuff dying stabbing victim
events.description: Bodycam footage shows English police handcuffing 18-year-old Henry Nowak after he said he was stabbed and could not breathe moments before dying.

events.title: Israeli official says EU sanctions reveal antisemitism hiding behind 'socially acceptable mask'
events.description: Israel's Deputy Foreign Minister accuses the EU of disguising antisemitism as anti-Zionism after sanctions hit Israeli civil society organizations.

events.title: Iranians speak out over possible Trump-regime deal
events.description: Iranians inside Iran express fear and frustration that a potential U.S.-Iran deal could leave the Islamic Republic intact despite economic devastation.

events.title: US ally answers Trump's call on Strait of Hormuz: 'Part of a diplomatic effort'
events.description: Romania allowed access to military bases for air refueling and defensive activities amid U.S.-Iran tensions, its foreign minister tells Fox News Digital.

events.title: Hidden tunnel discovered in Tijuana may have supported cross-border trafficking operations
events.description: Authorities uncover a 265-meter underground tunnel near the U.S.-Mexico border equipped with lighting, ventilation and an electronic transport system.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
events.description: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
